title: Waymo DC public-service gate and aggregate 2027 safety market drafts
warning: Draft only. No market has been created and no bet has been placed.

external_gate_market.question: Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?

markets.question: If Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027, how many traffic fatalities will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
This market forecasts DC's aggregate road-safety outcome in 2027 conditional on the existing Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolving YES. DC's tracked safety metrics include traffic fatalities, serious injuries, fatalities per vehicle mile traveled, pedestrian fatalities, bicyclist fatalities, and pedestrian/bicyclist injuries. This draft uses total traffic fatalities as the primary high-salience outcome.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if the linked Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolves NO or N/A. If that market resolves YES, resolve to the number of traffic fatalities in DC during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Vision Zero, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Highway Safety Plan data. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [Manifold: Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in)
- [Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.](https://waymo.com/blog/2025/03/next-stop-for-waymo-one-washingtondc/)
- [DC Vision Zero Traffic Fatalities and Injury Crashes](https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/district-of-columbia-vision-zero-traffic-fatalities-and-injury-crashes)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 38
- Rationale: DC's recent fatality baseline is in the low-to-mid 30s on multi-year averages, but year-to-year variance is high. If Waymo serves the general public before 2027, any aggregate effect in 2027 is likely modest because deployment scale and substitution from human-driven trips are uncertain.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
markets.resolution_rule: Resolve N/A if the linked Manifold market `Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?` resolves NO or N/A. If that market resolves YES, resolve to the number of traffic fatalities in DC during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Vision Zero, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Highway Safety Plan data. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

sources.title: Manifold: Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?
Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.
DC Vision Zero Traffic Fatalities and Injury Crashes

source_forecast.rationale: DC's recent fatality baseline is in the low-to-mid 30s on multi-year averages, but year-to-year variance is high. If Waymo serves the general public before 2027, any aggregate effect in 2027 is likely modest because deployment scale and substitution from human-driven trips are uncertain.

notes: This market estimates aggregate DC safety, not Waymo crash involvement.
Pair with the linked-gate NO conditional market to compare expected citywide outcomes.

markets.question: If Waymo does not serve the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027, how many traffic fatalities will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
This market forecasts DC's aggregate road-safety outcome in 2027 conditional on the existing Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolving NO. It is the counterpart to the YES-conditional traffic-fatalities market.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if the linked Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolves YES or N/A. If that market resolves NO, resolve to the number of traffic fatalities in DC during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Vision Zero, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Highway Safety Plan data. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [Manifold: Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in)
- [DC Vision Zero Traffic Fatalities and Injury Crashes](https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/district-of-columbia-vision-zero-traffic-fatalities-and-injury-crashes)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 40
- Rationale: Without public Waymo service before 2027, 2027 fatalities should mostly follow DC's existing traffic-safety trend and annual volatility. The estimate is slightly above the YES-conditional case because public Waymo service could substitute for some higher-risk human driving, but the expected difference is small.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._

markets.question: If Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027, how many serious injuries in traffic crashes will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
Traffic fatalities are highly salient but noisy. Serious injuries are a broader aggregate safety metric that DC uses in highway safety planning and crash reporting. This market forecasts DC's 2027 serious-injury count conditional on the existing Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolving YES.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if the linked Manifold market [Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in) resolves NO or N/A. If that market resolves YES, resolve to the number of serious injuries in DC traffic crashes during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Highway Safety Plan, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Vision Zero data. If the source labels the metric as major injuries or KABCO A injuries, use that official serious-injury-equivalent count.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [Manifold: Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/waymo-serves-the-general-public-in)
- [Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.](https://waymo.com/blog/2025/03/next-stop-for-waymo-one-washingtondc/)
- [DC Crashes in DC metadata](https://opendata.dc.gov/documents/DCGIS::crashes-in-dc/about)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 315
- Rationale: DC's recent serious-injury baseline is in the low 300s. Public Waymo service before 2027 could slightly reduce risk if it substitutes for human-driven trips, but 2027 deployment scale and changes in total vehicle miles traveled are uncertain.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
markets.resolution_rule: Resolve N/A if the linked Manifold market `Waymo serves the general public in Washington, D.C. before 2027?` resolves NO or N/A. If that market resolves YES, resolve to the number of serious injuries in DC traffic crashes during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Highway Safety Plan, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Vision Zero data. If the source labels the metric as major injuries or KABCO A injuries, use that official serious-injury-equivalent count.

Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.

source_forecast.rationale: DC's recent serious-injury baseline is in the low 300s. Public Waymo service before 2027 could slightly reduce risk if it substitutes for human-driven trips, but 2027 deployment scale and changes in total vehicle miles traveled are uncertain.

notes: This market estimates aggregate DC safety, not Waymo crash involvement.
Use the same official serious-injury definition across both conditional markets.
